zh.Wolverton, Buckinghamshire, England: 英國巴金哈姆爾沃爾維頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Moore於1408年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾沃爾維頓時，他的父親John Moore26歲，母親Margery Cambrae22歲。他和Lady Mary Perkes Havens至少有1個女兒。他去世於1470年在他的家鄉享年62歲。

zh-hans.Wolverton, Buckinghamshire, England: 英国巴金哈姆爾沃爾維頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Moore于1408年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾沃爾維頓时，他的父亲John Moore26岁，母亲Margery Cambrae22岁。他和Lady Mary Perkes Havens至少有1个女儿。他去世于1470年在他的家乡享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: 1  Scottish, English:  see  Moore  .

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Moore was born in 1408, in Wolverton, Buckinghamshire, England, his father, John Moore, was 26 and his mother, Margery Cambrae, was 22. He had at least 1 daughter with Lady Mary Perkes Havens. He died in 1470, in his hometown, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Moore est né en 1408, à Wolverton, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, John Moore, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Margery Cambrae, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Lady Mary Perkes Havens. Il est décédé en 1470, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Moore 1408 in Wolverton, Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Moore 26 und seine Mutter Margery Cambrae 22. Er hatte mit Lady Mary Perkes Havens mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1470 im Alter von 62 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Moore è nato il nel 1408 nel Wolverton, Buckinghamshire, England, il suo padre, John Moore, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Margery Cambrae, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Lady Mary Perkes Havens. Morì nel 1470, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.Wolverton, Buckinghamshire, England: イギリスブクインガムシュアイアウオルバートン
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Mooreが1408年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアウオルバートンで生まれた時，父John Mooreは26歳，母Margery Cambraeは22歳であった。彼はLady Mary Perkes Havensとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1470年に故郷で62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Moore(이)가 1408년 영국 벅잉함실르 울버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Moore(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery Cambrae(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Lady Mary Perkes Havens(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1470년 그의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Moore nasceu em 1408, em Wolverton, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Moore, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Margery Cambrae, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Lady Mary Perkes Havens. Ele faleceu em 1470, em sua cidade natal, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Moore родился в 1408 г. в местности волвиртон, буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Moore было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Cambrae, 22. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Lady Mary Perkes Havens. Он умер в 1470 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Moore nació en 1408, en Wolverton, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Moore, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Margery Cambrae, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Lady Mary Perkes Havens. Murió en 1470, en su pueblo, a la edad de 62 años.
